text: 25. FAILURE TO PERFORM: Should  Subcontractor  at  any  time  fail  to  supply  a  sufficient  number  of  skilled  workmen,  or  a sufficient  quantity  of  equipment  or  materials  of  proper  quality,  or  fail  in  any  respect  to  prosecute  the  work  covered  by  this subcontract with promptness and diligence, or fail to perform work of the quality required by the Subcontract Documents, or fail to  promptly  correct  defective  or  nonconforming  work,  or  fail  to  comply  with  all  applicable  laws,  codes,  ordinances  and regulations,  or  fail  in  the  performance  of  any  of  the  agreements  herein  contained,  or  should  any  workmen  performing  work covered by this subcontract engage in a strike or other stoppage, or cease to work due to picketing or other such activity, such event shall constitute an event of default under this subcontract, and Contractor may, in any of such events at its option, after forty-eight  hours  written  notice  to  Subcontractor,  provide  any  such  labor  and  materials,  and  deduct  the  cost  thereof  from  any money then due or thereafter to become due Subcontractor, or, in any of such events, Contractor, may, at its option, a n d without forty-eight hours written notice, terminate the employment of Subcontractor for all or part of the work under this subcontract, and shall have the right to enter upon the Project premises and take possession, for the purpose of completing the work hereunder, of all the materials, tools, facilities, appliances and equipment thereon, and to finish the work and provide the materials, therefore, either  with  its  own  employees  or  other  subcontractors;  and  in  case  of  such  discontinuance  of  the  employment  by  Contractor, Subcontractor shall not be entitled  to  receive  any  further  payments  under  this  subcontract  or  otherwise,  but  shall  nevertheless remain  liable  for  any  damages  which  Contractor  incurs.  If  the  expenses  incurred  by  Contractor  in  completing  the  work  shall exceed  the  unpaid  balance,  Subcontractor  shall  pay  the  difference  to  Contractor,  along  with  any  and  all  other  damages  and attorneys' fees incurred by Contractor as a result of Subcontractor's default. Contractor shall have a lien upon all materials, tools, facilities, appliances and equipment taken possession of to secure the payment hereof. Subcontractor shall be liable to Contractor for all costs and damages incurred by Contractor due to the default or failure of performance by Subcontractor, including but not limited  to  the  failure  of  Subcontractor  to  keep  the  progress  of  work  up  to  that  of  Contractor  or  other  trades,  or  the  failure  to execute  its  work  as  directed  by  Contractor.  In  the  event  of  any  termination  for  default,  Subcontractor  agrees  to  execute  any assignment necessary to make available to Contractor and the Owner the rights of Subcontractor under all purchase orders and subcontracts related to the sublet work. Contractor will credit Subcontractor's  account with the value of the materials and supplies so  used,  but  there  will  be  no  credit  for  rent  on  equipment.  Subcontractor  will  reimburse  Contractor  in  Harris  County,  Texas, promptly and within no more than ten (10) days from the date of Contractor's demand, to the extent that Contractor's damages and expenses, including attorney's fees, in completing the sublet work and proceeding under this article exceeds the balance which would have become due to Subcontractor under this subcontract had Subcontractor completed the sublet work in accordance with the Subcontract Documents. If Contractor's damages and expenses, including but not limited to attorneys' fees incurred, is less than  such  amount,  then  Contractor  will  pay  the  difference  to  Subcontractor.  Subcontractor  hereby  waives  all  claims  against Contractor for profits, rent or equipment or other damages related to any proceeding which Contractor institutes under this article. The parties agree that any determination by Contractor as to the adequacy of Subcontractor's performance are difficult to make and must be made under pressing circumstances, and Subcontractor agrees to be bound in accordance with this article by the Contractor's discretion in light of the circumstances confronting Contractor at the time such a decision is made. If any termination by Contractor for default under this paragraph 25 is found by any arbitrator(s) or court to be wrongful or otherwise improper, such termination shall  be  deemed a termination for convenience under paragraph 24 above, and Subcontractor's sole and exclusive remedy for such termination shall be the payments provided for in paragraph 24 above.
